aboutHome.label: [ ຂ້ອຍແມ່ນໃຜ ]
aboutHome.title: ກ່ຽວກັບຂ້ອຍ
aboutHome.intro: ຂ້ອຍແມ່ນ Maximiliano Torres, ນັກພັດທະນາ Software Full-Stack ຢູ່ວຽງຈັນ ປະເທດລາວ, ມຸ່ງເນັ້ນການພັດທະນາ Web Application ທີ່ແຂງແຮງ ແລະ ຂະຫຍາຍໄດ້.
aboutHome.description: ຂ້ອຍຊ່ວຍທຸລະກິດປ່ຽນແນວຄິດໃຫ້ເປັນເຄື່ອງມືດິຈິຕອນທີ່ເຊື່ອຖືໄດ້ — ຈາກ dashboard ພາຍໃນ ຫາ platform ຂະໜາດໃຫຍ່ — ໂດຍເນັ້ນໂຄງສ້າງທີ່ສະອາດ, ປະສິດທິພາບ ແລະ UX ທີ່ດີ.
aboutHome.imageAlt: Maximiliano Torres - ນັກພັດທະນາ Full Stack
aboutHome.learnMore: ຮູ້ຈັກຂ້ອຍເພີ່ມ

about.heroLabel: [ ໂປຣໄຟລ໌ນັກພັດທະນາ ]
about.heroTitle: ກ່ຽວກັບຂ້ອຍ
about.heroSubtitle: ຜູ້ຊ່ຽວຊານ React & Next.js | Full-Stack Developer ຕັ້ງແຕ່ 2017

bio.title: ພັດທະນາລະບົບເວັບທີ່ເຊື່ອຖືໄດ້ ແລະ ຂະຫຍາຍໄດ້
bio.p1: ຂ້ອຍແມ່ນ Maximiliano Torres, Full-Stack Developer ຢູ່ເອຊີຕາເວັນອອກສຽງໃຕ້, ຊ່ຽວຊານໃນການພັດທະນາ Web Application ດ້ວຍ React ແລະ Next.js.
bio.p2: ຂ້ອຍອອກແບບ ແລະ ພັດທະນາໂຊລູຊັນດິຈິຕອນເພື່ອອັດຕະໂນມັດ workflow ແລະ ຊ່ວຍໃຫ້ທຸລະກິດເຕີບໂຕ.
bio.p3: ນອກເໜືອຈາກການພັດທະນາ, ຂ້ອຍຍັງມີປະສົບການສອນດ້ານ IT ແລະ Web Design ທີ່ມະຫາວິທະຍາໄລໃນຈີນ ແລະ ລາວ.

tools.title: ເຄື່ອງມື & DevOps

cta.services: ເບິ່ງບໍລິການ

services.subtitleSecondary: ຕັ້ງແຕ່ MVP ໄປຫາ production, ຂ້ອຍຊ່ວຍໃຫ້ founder ປ່ຽນໄອເດຍໃຫ້ເປັນແອັບເວັບທີ່ເຊື່ອຖືໄດ້ ແລະ ຂະຫຍາຍໄດ້.

customWebApps.title: ພັດທະນາຜະລິດຕະພັນ SaaS
customWebApps.valueStatement: ພັດທະນາ SaaS ທັນສະໄໝແບບ end-to-end ສໍາລັບຜູ້ໃຊ້ຫຼາຍລາຍ.
customWebApps.desc: ຂ້ອຍສ້າງລະບົບທີ່ມີໂຄງສ້າງດີ ເພື່ອຮອງຮັບການເຕີບໂຕ, ປະສິດທິພາບ ແລະ ການບໍາລຸງຮັກສາໃນໄລຍະຍາວ.
details: ສະຖາປັດຕະຍະກໍາ SaaS ແບບ multi-tenant
ລະບົບ authentication ແລະ role-based access control
Admin dashboard ແລະ user portal
ການເຊື່ອມຕໍ່ backend ແບບ API-driven
ການ deploy ທີ່ພ້ອມໃຊ້ງານຈິງ
customWebApps.tools: Next.js · React · TypeScript · PostgreSQL · REST APIs

businessSystems.title: Marketplace ແລະ Multi-Sided Platforms
businessSystems.valueStatement: Platform ທີ່ເຊື່ອມຕໍ່ຜູ້ໃຊ້, ຜູ້ຂາຍ ຫຼື ຜູ້ໃຫ້ບໍລິການ.
businessSystems.desc: ອອກແບບໃຫ້ຂະຫຍາຍໄດ້, ປະສິດທິພາບດີ ແລະ ຄວບຄຸມການດໍາເນີນງານໄດ້ຊັດເຈນ.
details: ລະບົບຈັດການຜູ້ຂາຍ ແລະ ຜູ້ໃຊ້
Workflow ດ້ານທຸລະກໍາ
ລະບົບ review ແລະ rating
Dashboard ສໍາລັບ moderation
ສະຖາປັດຕະຍະກໍາ platform ທີ່ຂະຫຍາຍໄດ້
businessSystems.tools: Next.js · React · TypeScript · PostgreSQL · REST APIs

pwaApps.title: MVP Development ສໍາລັບ Startup
pwaApps.valueStatement: ເປີດຕົວໄວ. ທົດສອບໄວ. ຂະຫຍາຍໄດ້ມັ່ນໃຈ.
pwaApps.desc: ຂ້ອຍຊ່ວຍ founder ສ້າງ MVP ທີ່ lean ແຕ່ແຂງແຮງ ດ້ວຍພື້ນຖານທາງເທັກນິກທີ່ດີ.
details: ໂຄງສ້າງ MVP ແບບໄວ
Codebase ທີ່ສະອາດ ແລະ ບໍາລຸງຮັກສາງ່າຍ
Frontend ທີ່ເນັ້ນປະສິດທິພາບ
ໂຄງສ້າງລະບົບທີ່ຂະຫຍາຍໄດ້
ຄໍາແນະນໍາດ້ານເທັກນິກເຊີງຍຸດທະສາດ
pwaApps.tools: Next.js · React · TypeScript · PostgreSQL · REST APIs

referral.label: [ ໂຄງການແນະນຳ ]
referral.title: ແນະນຳລູກຄ້າ.
referral.desc: ຮູ້ຈັກໃຜຕ້ອງການເວັບໄຊຫຼືການເຊື່ອມຕໍ່ລະບົບ? ສົ່ງພວກເຂົາມາຫາຂ້ອຍ — ຂ້ອຍຈະຕອບແທນຄຸນ. ບໍ່ຕ້ອງຕື່ມຟອມ, ບໍ່ຕ້ອງຜ່ານຂະບວນການ. ພຽງແຕ່ສົ່ງຂໍ້ຄວາມ.
steps: ແນະນຳໝູ່ໃຫ້ຮູ້ກ່ຽວກັບບໍລິການຂອງຂ້ອຍ
ເຂົາຕິດຕໍ່ Maxon ແລະ ບອກຊື່ທ່ານ
ເຂົາເຊັນ — ທ່ານຮັບລາງວັນ

yangoArticle.lede: Shortly after the end of the Covid Era, I spent time teaching web development at Yango University in the city of Fuzhou, Fujian. I had always wanted to try life and work in China.

curriculum.label: [ THE CURRICULUM ]
curriculum.heading: Teaching the Foundations of the Web
curriculum.p1: My role was to introduce students to the fundamentals of web development.
topics: The client–server model behind web applications
curriculum.p3: Instead of only teaching theory, I tried to keep things practical as much as possible, classes needed to be fun, that is what motivates students the most. The goal was always to get students building something they could see working in the browser.
curriculum.p4: Even a simple landing page or interactive UI was enough to get people excited about programming.

yangoArticle.figStudentPresenting: Student presenting their project to the class

challenge.label: [ THE CHALLENGE ]
challenge.heading: Teaching in a Different Culture
challenge.p1: Teaching in China came with some unique challenges.
challenge.p2: Language differences meant that long technical explanations didn't always work well, I taught my classes in English, and not every student could understand. So I relied heavily on visual demonstrations, live coding, lots of diagrams, and examples.
challenge.p3: One thing I noticed quickly is that code itself becomes the common language. Once students start experimenting and seeing results in the browser, the barrier disappears.

insight.label: [ THE INSIGHT ]
insight.heading: Why Teaching Makes You a Better Developer
insight.p1: Teaching programming forces you to understand things at a deeper level.
insight.p2: When you have to explain concepts like:
concepts: Why CSS layouts behave the way they do
insight.p3: You quickly discover which parts you truly understand and which parts you don't.
insight.p4: That process made me a better developer in several ways:
insight.p5: All of those skills matter just as much as writing code.

perspective.label: [ PERSPECTIVE ]
perspective.heading: A Global Industry
perspective.p1: One thing that stood out to me while teaching in China was how global software development really is.
perspective.p2: Students in Fuzhou were learning the same core technologies used by developers everywhere: web standards, JavaScript, APIs, and modern development tools.
perspective.p3: The problems developers work on are surprisingly similar no matter where you are in the world.
perspective.p4: But yeah, infrastructure is quite different and confusing.

reflection.label: [ REFLECTION ]
reflection.heading: Looking Back
reflection.p1: Teaching web development was an experience that stayed with me. It reinforced something that I still believe strongly: strong fundamentals and clear thinking matter more than any particular framework or tool.
reflection.p2: And those fundamentals are the same whether you are teaching in a classroom or building real software.
reflection.calloutText: Strong fundamentals and clear thinking matter more than any particular framework or tool.

cta.label: [ MORE ARTICLES ]
cta.heading: Keep Reading
cta.subtitle: Check out more technical notes, guides, and lessons learned from my experience.

orderbridgeArticle.titleMain: How I Built OrderBridge: Solving the
orderbridgeArticle.lede: A few months ago, a client came to me with a problem that was simple to describe and it seemed quite straightforward. They needed to bridge the gap between major delivery platforms and their own POS system with an automated OAuth 2.0 handshake.
orderbridgeArticle.introP1Pre: This project started as a real client brief. The request came in Spanish:
orderbridgeArticle.introP1Post: In short: they needed to connect their clients' delivery accounts to their POS system through StreamOrder's partner API — automatically, via OAuth, with no manual intervention. I analysed the requirements, read through the StreamOrder partner documentation, and got to work.

brief.label: [ THE BRIEF ]
brief.heading: The Client Proposal
brief.p1: Before writing a single line of code, I put together a formal proposal. It forced clarity on both sides — the client understood exactly what they were getting, and I understood exactly what I was building.
brief.descriptionText: This project establishes a secure, automated "Digital Bridge" — a Middleware API between the StreamOrder aggregator and the client's Point of Sale (POS) system, enabling delivery orders to flow automatically into the POS with no manual intervention.
brief.objectiveText: Implement OAuth 2.0 as the authentication layer, ensuring data remains secure while allowing orders to sync automatically from delivery platforms into the POS.

objectives.label: Secure Connection
objectives.desc: OAuth handshake — no shared passwords, data protected by encrypted digital keys.

objectives.label: Automatic Sync
objectives.desc: Every DoorDash and delivery platform order appears in the POS instantly.

weeks.title: Security & The Digital Handshake
weeks.result: StreamOrder officially linked; data protected by encrypted OAuth keys.

weeks.title: The Automation Bridge
weeks.result: Translator and webhook listener live in a test environment — orders captured and translated instantly.

weeks.title: Dashboard & Live Launch
weeks.result: Bridge active, orders printing in the POS, live monitoring dashboard delivered.

weeks.title: Refinement & Monitoring
weeks.result: 7-day live monitoring window; API changes from StreamOrder or delivery platforms patched immediately.

deliverables.name: OAuth 2.0 Integration
deliverables.desc: Authentication layer connecting all accounts without sharing private passwords.

deliverables.name: Real-Time Order Injection
deliverables.desc: Orders detected via webhook and pushed directly into the POS within seconds.

deliverables.name: Automated Translator
deliverables.desc: Intelligent menu mapping ensuring every item, modifier, and customer note matches the POS format.

deliverables.name: Automatic Token Refresh
deliverables.desc: Self-maintaining security — OAuth tokens renew automatically so the connection never expires.

deliverables.name: Reliability Monitoring
deliverables.desc: Error-tracking that alerts immediately if an order fails to sync.

deliverables.name: Live Connection Dashboard
deliverables.desc: Web view to monitor bridge status and verify the most recent successful syncs.

prerequisites: StreamOrder Developer access with Partner API enabled.
POS developer portal credentials with write permissions.
Menu / SKU list exported from the POS (CSV or Excel).
A named technical point of contact authorised to approve the OAuth handshake.

painPoint.label: [ THE PAIN POINT ]
painPoint.heading: Keeping Track of All the Orders
painPoint.p1: Before we automated this, the workflow was boring and painful. Most busy kitchens have a counter full of tablets from different delivery apps.
listItems: Read the order off a tablet.
Manually type it into the POS.
Hope they didn't miss a "no onions" modifier or something.
painPoint.p2: In a quiet shop, it's boring. In a kitchen doing 80+ orders a day, it's a painful operational problem.
painPoint.mathPre: 50 orders a day × 2.5 minutes of manual entry × $18/hr =
painPoint.mathPost: to a task that shouldn't involve a human at all.

orderbridgeArticle.figOrders: Orders page — filterable, sortable, real-time status updates

engineering.label: [ ENGINEERING ]
engineering.heading: Engineering the Pipeline
engineering.p1: Once the ROI was clear, I mapped out the pipeline. The logic needed to be bulletproof:
engineering.figDiagram: High-level flow: delivery platforms → OrderBridge → restaurant POS

steps.title: Ingest delivery webhooks
steps.desc: Receive HTTPS POST from each delivery platform.

steps.title: Verify signatures
steps.desc: HMAC-SHA256 check — security before anything else.

steps.title: Translate schemas
steps.desc: Map disparate JSON payloads into a unified POS format.

steps.title: Inject the order
steps.desc: POST to the POS API with exponential-backoff retry.

steps.title: Broadcast updates
steps.desc: WebSocket push to the live dashboard — no polling.

process.label: [ THE PROCESS ]
process.heading: Writing the Spec First
process.p1: I started with a Software Requirements Specification (SRS) before writing a single line of code. It's much easier to fix a mistake in a document than to rewrite production code later. The SRS forced both me and the client to agree on exactly what "done" looked like — no ambiguity, no scope creep.

requirements.label: FR-01
requirements.title: OAuth 2.0 Server
requirements.desc: System must act as an OAuth 2.0 provider — issuing, validating, and refreshing tokens per RFC 6749.

requirements.label: FR-02
requirements.title: Webhook Ingestion
requirements.desc: System must accept HTTPS POST webhooks from delivery platforms and verify each request with HMAC-SHA256 signature validation.

requirements.label: FR-03
requirements.title: Schema Translation
requirements.desc: System must map all incoming delivery payloads to a unified POS format, with graceful failure and logging on unmapped SKUs.

requirements.label: FR-04
requirements.title: POS Injection
requirements.desc: System must POST translated orders to the POS API with exponential-backoff retry on failure — no order dropped silently.

requirements.label: NFR-01
requirements.title: Real-Time Updates
requirements.desc: Dashboard must reflect order status changes via WebSocket push. No polling.

requirements.label: NFR-02
requirements.title: Token Persistence
requirements.desc: OAuth tokens must survive server restarts, stored encrypted at rest.

orderbridgeArticle.figDashboard: Live dashboard — real-time order feed with status tracking

techStack.label: [ TECH STACK ]
techStack.heading: The Tech Stack
techStack.subtitle: What tools would I use for this kind of project:

items.name: Node.js & TypeScript
items.body: I am sure that Python would've also worked wonders here. Personally I prefer working with node modules and npm over pip and virtual environments.

items.name: Fastify (Not Express)
items.body: Express is the legacy default, but Fastify is built for modern performance. Built-in JSON schema validation, lower overhead. For a middleware API where every millisecond counts, Fastify wins.

deployment.label: [ DEPLOYMENT ]
deployment.heading: Infrastructure
deployment.p1: The three services are independently deployed — each with its own host, its own domain, and its own responsibility. This mirrors how a real production integration would be structured.

services.detail: Real-time monitoring UI — edge-deployed, globally available.
Core middleware engine with the OAuth server, webhook ingestion, schema translator, and POS injector. PostgreSQL stores OAuth tokens, menu mappings, and order logs.
Isolated POS simulator running as a completely separate service — its own OAuth consent flow, its own order terminal.

deployment.whySeparate: Keeping MockPOS on its own Railway service wasn't just convenient — it was the point. It proves the integration works across a real network boundary, not just between two functions in the same process.

hardParts.label: [ THE HARD PARTS ]
hardParts.heading: Translation and Trust
hardParts.p1: The Translator was the hardest part of this project. DoorDash, Uber Eats, and GrabFood all have wildly different ideas of how to describe a "Cheeseburger with no pickles." Mapping these inconsistent payloads to a standardised POS SKU while maintaining a MenuMapping table required deep edge-case handling. If the mapping fails, the system has to fail gracefully — not drop the order into a void.
hardParts.p2: Then there was the OAuth 2.0 Server. It's one thing to use OAuth; it's another to be the provider. Building the authorization endpoints, token logic, and encrypted storage according to the RFC specs took more gray hair than the rest of the app combined.

orderbridgeArticle.figMockPos: Phing Kai Kitchen [POS Simulator] — orders arriving automatically from OrderBridge

portfolio.label: [ THE PORTFOLIO BUILD ]
portfolio.heading: Introducing OrderBridge
portfolio.p1: I enjoyed the architecture of the client project so much that I built OrderBridge — a refined, standalone version for my portfolio. It's not just a copy; it's an evolution. A complete end-to-end demo that simulates the entire flow without needing real-world infrastructure.

repos.name: Dashboard
repos.body: Real-time monitoring and control UI for the OrderBridge system.

repos.name: POS Simulator (MockPOS)
repos.body: A secondary app representing a fictional restaurant called Phing Kai Kitchen. It acts as the 'target' POS, complete with its own OAuth consent screen and live order terminal.

orderbridgeArticle.figSimulator: Simulator — fire test orders from any delivery platform with one click

sourceCode.label: [ SOURCE CODE ]
sourceCode.heading: The Complete Codebase
sourceCode.subtitle: All three core components are open-source and available on GitHub:

repos.title: OrderBridge API
repos.desc: Core middleware engine with OAuth 2.0, webhook ingestion, and schema translation.

repos.title: OrderBridge Dashboard
repos.desc: Real-time monitoring UI with live order feed and connection status.

repos.title: MockPOS Server
repos.desc: POS simulator demonstrating the complete integration flow.

liveDemos.label: [ LIVE DEMOS ]
liveDemos.heading: Try It Out
liveDemos.subtitle: Both services are live and ready to use:

demos.name: OrderBridge Dashboard
demos.desc: Real-time monitoring interface. Watch orders flow through the pipeline as they arrive.

demos.name: MockPOS Server
demos.desc: Live orders page from the fictional Phing Kai Kitchen POS system. See orders injected in real time.

cta.label: [ SEE IT IN ACTION ]
cta.heading: Ready to Explore?
cta.subtitle: Dive into the source code, watch the live systems in action, or check out the full case study.

orderbridgeCaseStudy.tagline: ລະບົບ middleware ທີ່ເຊື່ອມຕໍ່ platform delivery ໄປຫາ POS ຂອງຮ້ານ — ລຶບການປ້ອນຂໍ້ມູນດ້ວຍມືທັງໝົດ.
orderbridgeCaseStudy.readBuildArticle: ອ່ານບົດຄວາມ
orderbridgeCaseStudy.diagramCaption: ຮູບແບບລວມ: delivery platforms → OrderBridge → restaurant POS

outcomes.label: ການປະມວນຜົນ order ຈາກຕົ້ນຈົນຈົບ
Delivery platforms ທີ່ຮອງຮັບ
POS systems ທີ່ເຊື່ອມຕໍ່
ຂັ້ນຕອນດ້ວຍມືທີ່ຕ້ອງການ

problem.p1: ທຸກຄັ້ງທີ່ order ມາຈາກ DoorDash ຫຼື Uber Eats, ພະນັກງານຕ້ອງອ່ານຈາກ tablet ແລ້ວພິມໃສ່ POS ດ້ວຍມື. ທີ 50 orders ຕໍ່ວັນ, ນັ້ນແມ່ນເວລາ 2 ຊົ່ວໂມງທຸກໆວັນ.
problem.p2: ການປ້ອນດ້ວຍມືນຳມາຊຶ່ງຄວາມຜິດພາດ. modifier ທີ່ພາດ, ປະລິມານຜິດ, item ທີ່ຂາດ.
problem.p3: ຄະນິດສາດ: 50 orders × 2.5 ນາທີ × $18/ຊົ່ວໂມງ =
problem.p3Highlight: ເສຍ $1,100+ ທຸກເດືອນ
problem.p3End: ສຳລັບວຽກທີ່ຄວນ automate.

solution.p1: OrderBridge ຢູ່ລະຫວ່າງ delivery platforms ແລະ POS. ເມື່ອ order ມາຮອດ, ມັນຮັບ webhook, ແປ payload ໄປເປັນຮູບແບບ POS ແລະ inject order ໂດຍອັດຕະໂນມັດ — ໃນເວລາຕ່ຳກວ່າ 800ms.

howItWorks.sectionLabel: [ ວິທີທີ່ໃຊ້ງານ ]

steps.title: Webhook Reception
steps.desc: ແຕ່ລະ delivery platform ສົ່ງ HTTPS POST ໄປຫາ webhook endpoint. Server ກວດສອບລາຍເຊັນ HMAC-SHA256 ແລະ deduplicate ຕາມ order ID.

steps.title: Translation
steps.desc: Handler ສະເພາະ platform ແປ schema delivery ໄປເປັນ POS payload ມາດຕະຖານ ໂດຍໃຊ້ MenuMapping table.

steps.title: Injection
steps.desc: Payload ທີ່ແປແລ້ວຖືກ POST ໄປຫາ POS API ດ້ວຍ exponential backoff retry. ເມື່ອ order ລົ້ມ, ມັນຖືກ flag ສຳລັບ manual review.

steps.title: Real-Time Sync
steps.desc: ທຸກການປ່ຽນແປງ state ຖືກ persist ໄປ PostgreSQL ແລະ broadcast ຜ່ານ WebSocket ໄປຫາ dashboard — ບໍ່ polling.

images.alt: OrderBridge dashboard showing live order feed
images.caption: Live dashboard — real-time order feed with status tracking
Orders page — filterable, sortable, CSV export

images.alt: MockPOS mock terminal showing injected orders
images.caption: MockPOS terminal — orders arriving automatically from OrderBridge
Simulator — fire test orders from any delivery platform

items.name: React 19 + Vite
items.reason: Fast dev experience, React Query for server state, no SSR overhead needed for a dashboard.

items.name: Fastify + TypeScript
items.reason: Lower overhead than Express, schema-based validation built in, excellent TypeScript support.

items.name: PostgreSQL + Prisma
items.reason: Relational data fits the order/token/mapping schema well. Prisma keeps migrations safe and typed.

items.name: WebSockets (ws)
items.reason: Native WebSocket — no Socket.io overhead for a single broadcast channel.

items.name: OAuth 2.0 + AES-256
items.reason: Industry-standard POS auth flow. Tokens encrypted at rest before writing to the database.

items.name: HMAC-SHA256
items.reason: Webhook signature verification — only legitimate delivery platform payloads are processed.

teaching-web-development-china-yango-uni.title: ສອນພັດທະນາເວັບໃນຈີນ @ Yango University
teaching-web-development-china-yango-uni.excerpt: ການສອນ HTML, CSS ແລະ JavaScript ຂັ້ນພື້ນຖານໃນເມືອງ Fuzhou ປະເທດຈີນ ສອນໃຫ້ຂ້ອຍເປັນນັກພັດທະນາທີ່ດີຂຶ້ນ. ບົດຮຽນກ່ຽວກັບການສຶກສາຂ້າມວັດທະນະທຳ ແລະ ເຫດຜົນທີ່ທ່ານສາດຂັ້ນພື້ນຖານມີຄວາມສຳຄັນຫຼາຍກວ່າ framework.

how-i-built-orderbridge.title: ຂ້ອຍສ້າງ OrderBridge ແນວໃດ: ແກ້ບັນຫາ Middleware ລະຫວ່າງ Delivery ແລະ POS
how-i-built-orderbridge.excerpt: ການສ້າງລະບົບ middleware OAuth 2.0 ທີ່ເຊື່ອມ DoorDash, Uber Eats ແລະ platform delivery ໄປຫາ POS ຂອງຮ້ານອາຫານ. ການວິເຄາະເທັກນິກຄ້ານ webhook ingestion, schema translation ແລະ real-time order sync.

laomaitravelCaseStudy.tagline: ເວັບໄຊທ໌ທ່ອງທ່ຽວສອງພາສາ (EN/TH) ສ້າງດ້ວຍ SEO, analytics ທີ່ບໍ່ໃຊ້ cookie ແລະ ລະບົບຮັບການຈອງ — ບໍ່ຕ້ອງການ backend.

laomaitravelCaseStudy.heroImageAlt: Lao Mai Travel hero section — temple at dusk with navy and gold design

outcomes.label: ເສັ້ນທາງສອງພາສາ
HTML ທີ່ render ຈາກ server ເພື່ອ SEO
ປ້າຍ cookie ຫຼື popup
ການເຂົ້າເຖິງ dashboard analytics ຂອງລູກຄ້າ

problem.p1: ກ່ອນທີ່ຈະມີເວັບໄຊທ໌ນີ້, Lao Mai Travel ບໍ່ຮູ້ວ່າເວັບໄຊທ໌ຂອງພວກເຂົາໃຊ້ງານໄດ້ຫຼືບໍ່. ບໍ່ມີຂໍ້ມູນກ່ຽວກັບຜູ້ເຂົ້າຊົມ, ແຫຼ່ງທີ່ມາ, ສິ່ງທີ່ເຂົາເຈົ້າເບິ່ງ ຫຼື ອຸປະກອນທີ່ໃຊ້.
problem.p2: ນອກຈາກນັ້ນ, ບໍ່ມີການ routing ສອງພາສາ ແລະ ບໍ່ມີລະບົບຮັບການຈອງ.
problem.p3: ສາມບັນຫາທີ່ຕ້ອງແກ້:
problems: ບໍ່ມີຂໍ້ມູນກ່ຽວກັບຜູ້ໃຊ້ ຫຼື ສິ່ງທີ່ໃຊ້ງານໄດ້
ບໍ່ມີການຮອງຮັບພາສາລາວ
ບໍ່ມີລະບົບຈອງ — ມີແຕ່ເບີໂທລະສັບ

design.p1: Brief ແມ່ນ premium, local, trustworthy. ບໍ່ແມ່ນສີສົດໃສທົ່ວໄປຂອງເວັບທ່ອງທ່ຽວ.
design.p2: Navy ແລະ gold — navy ເບິ່ງດູຈິງຈັງ ແລະ ເປັນມືອາຊີບ, gold ອ້າງອິງສະຖາປັດຕະຍະກຳວັດ ແລະ ມໍລະດົກພຸດທະສາສະໜາລາວ.
design.p3: Typography ສາມຊັ້ນ: Cinzel ສຳລັບ navigation, Cormorant Garamond ສຳລັບ headline, Montserrat ສຳລັບ body text.
design.p4: Hero section ໃຊ້ຮູບພາບວັດຕອນຕາເວັນຕົກ ພ້ອມ headline ທີ່ແຍກລະຫວ່າງສີຂາວ roman ແລະ gold italic.

nextjs.sectionLabel: [ ເປັນຫຍັງຈຶ່ງໃຊ້ NEXT.JS ]
nextjs.intro: ສາມເຫດຜົນ: SEO, bilingual routing, ບໍ່ຕ້ອງ maintain backend.

reasons.title: Render ດ້ວຍ server ເພື່ອ SEO
reasons.desc: ທຸກໜ້າ tour ແລະ destination render ເປັນ HTML ສົມບູນຈາກ server. ເຄື່ອງມືຄົ້ນຫາ index ທັງໝົດ — ບໍ່ຕ້ອງການ JavaScript.

reasons.title: Bilingual routing ທີ່ເປັນ first-class
reasons.desc: App Router ຈັດການ bilingual routing ຢ່າງສະອາດ — ທຸກໜ້າຢູ່ພາຍໃຕ້ app/[locale]/, ດັ່ງນັ້ນ /en ແລະ /th ແມ່ນ route ທີ່ແທ້ຈິງ. next-intl ຈັດການ translations.

reasons.title: ບໍ່ຕ້ອງ maintain backend
reasons.desc: Resend ຈັດການ email ຈາກຟອມຈອງ. Vercel ຈັດການ deployment — ບໍ່ຕ້ອງ configure server, ບໍ່ຕ້ອງ maintenance.

analytics.heading: ຈາກການຄາດຄະເນ ສູ່ ການຮູ້ຂໍ້ເທັດຈິງ.
analytics.p1: Umami track ທຸກການເຂົ້າຊົມແບບ real time — ໜ້າທີ່ເບິ່ງ, ເວລາໃນ site, ປະເທດທີ່ມາ, ແຫຼ່ງ referrer ແລະ ປະເພດອຸປະກອນ. ລູກຄ້າເຫັນທັງໝົດໃນ dashboard ທີ່ສະອາດ.
analytics.p2: Umami ຍັງ privacy-first — ບໍ່ມີ cookie, ບໍ່ເກັບຂໍ້ມູນສ່ວນຕົວ, GDPR compliant ໂດຍ default. ບໍ່ມີ banner ທີ່ລົບກວນ site.
analytics.p3: ລູກຄ້າເຂົ້າ dashboard ດ້ວຍລິ້ງດຽວ. ບໍ່ຕ້ອງ account, ບໍ່ຕ້ອງລະຫັດ, ບໍ່ຕ້ອງ subscription ລາຍເດືອນ.
analytics.questionsLabel: ຄຳຖາມທີ່ UMAMI ຕອບໄດ້ດຽວນີ້:
questions: ໜ້າ Bolaven Plateau tour ໄດ້ 600 views ຕໍ່ເດືອນ ແຕ່ແທບບໍ່ມີການຈອງ — ລາຄາສູງເກີນໄປ ຫຼື ໜ້ານັ້ນສ້າງຄວາມໄວ້ວາງໃຈບໍ່ພໍ?
65% ຂອງຜູ້ເຂົ້າຊົມໃຊ້ mobile — ຟອມຈອງງ່າຍພໍສຳລັບໂທລະສັບຫຼືບໍ່?
Traffic ເພີ່ມຂຶ້ນທຸກເດືອນພະຈິກ ແລະ ທັນວາ — ນັ້ນແມ່ນເວລາທີ່ຄວນ push promotions.
Traffic ມາຈາກ Australia ແລະ France ແຕ່ແທບບໍ່ມີຈາກ US — ຄວນ focus social media ຢູ່ໃສ?

images.caption: Dashboard ຫຼັກ — visits, bounce rate ແລະ session duration ແບບ real-time
ໜ້າ ແລະ ແຫຼ່ງ — tour ໃດໄດ້ traffic ແລະ ມາຈາກໃສ
ທີ່ຢູ່ຜູ້ເຂົ້າຊົມ — ຮູ້ວ່າຄວນ focus marketing ຢູ່ໃສ

images.alt: ໜ້າ destinations ຂອງ Lao Mai Travel
images.caption: Destinations — ສະຖານທີ່ທ່ອງທ່ຽວທີ່ຫຼົງລືມໃນລາວ

images.alt: ໜ້າ tour packages ຂອງ Lao Mai Travel
images.caption: Tour packages — trek, Mekong islands, homestay ທາງວັດທະນະທຳ

images.alt: ຟອມຈອງຂອງ Lao Mai Travel
images.caption: ຟອມຈອງ — ສົ່ງໄປຫາລູກຄ້າໂດຍກົງຜ່ານ Resend

images.alt: ເວັບໄຊທ໌ສະບັບພາສາໄທ
images.caption: ພາສາໄທ — /th route ພ້ອມ metadata ຂອງຕົນເອງ

items.name: Next.js 15 + App Router
items.reason: ໜ້າ render ຈາກ server ເພື່ອ SEO, locale-first routing ແລະ deployment ເທິງ Vercel.

items.name: TypeScript
items.reason: ການພັດທະນາທີ່ type-safe ທົ່ວ codebase — ຈັບ error ກ່ອນຈະ deploy.

items.name: CSS Modules
items.reason: Styles ທີ່ scoped ໂດຍບໍ່ມີ runtime overhead. ເໝາະສຳລັບ site ທີ່ design-heavy.

items.name: next-intl
items.reason: Bilingual routing first-class ພ້ອມ metadata ຕໍ່ locale ແລະ translations EN/TH ທີ່ຄົບຖ້ວນ.

items.name: Resend
items.reason: ຈັດການ email ຈາກຟອມຈອງ — ບໍ່ຕ້ອງການ backend server.

items.name: Umami
items.reason: Analytics ທີ່ privacy-first — ບໍ່ມີ cookie, GDPR compliant. ລູກຄ້າເຂົ້າ dashboard ດ້ວຍລິ້ງດຽວ.

items.name: Vercel
items.reason: Deployment ທີ່ zero-config ພ້ອມ auto previews ແລະ rollbacks ທັນທີ.

cta.sectionLabel: [ ເຮັດວຽກຮ່ວມກັນ ]
cta.heading: ຕ້ອງການເວັບໄຊທ໌ທີ່ໃຊ້ງານໄດ້ຈິງສຳລັບທຸລະກິດຂອງທ່ານ?
cta.subtitle: ຂ້ອຍສ້າງເວັບໄຊທ໌ສອງພາສາທີ່ SEO-first ສຳລັບທຸລະກິດທີ່ຕ້ອງການ presence ທາງ web ທີ່ແທ້ຈິງ.

notFound.label: ERROR // 404
notFound.title: ຫາຍໄປໃນ<highlight>ອະວະກາດ</highlight>
notFound.subtitle: ບໍ່ພົບໜ້າ
notFound.description: ໜ້ານີ້ໄດ້ລອຍໄປເກີນຂອບເຂດຈັກກະວານ. ອາດຈະຖືກຍ້າຍ, ລຶບ, ຫຼືບາງທີບໍ່ເຄີຍມີຢູ່.
notFound.ctaHome: ກັບໄປໜ້າຫຼັກ
